perturbation.original_text: 1.2. The term "Affiliate" means an entity controlling, controlled, or under common control with a party. For these purposes, "control" means: (a) the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct the management or policies of an entity, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise; or (b) the ownership, directly or indirectly, of at least fifty percent (50%) of the voting securities or other ownership interest of an entity.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2. The term "Affiliate" means an entity controlling, controlled, or under common control with a party. For these purposes, "control" means: (a) the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct the management or policies of an entity, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise; or (b) the ownership, directly or indirectly, of at least twenty-five percent (25%) of the voting securities or other ownership interest of an entity.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Affiliate' is changed in Section 1.2 to state that control requires only 25% ownership, but in Section 3.3, there is a reference to Affiliates where the understanding remains that Affiliate refers to the 50% threshold. This creates a contradiction regarding which ownership percentage defines an affiliate, affecting restrictions on T&B's actions. It creates an in-text contradiction when referring to the rights of an Affiliate in section 3.3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 T&B shall not, during the Term, grant any third party a license to use the Licensed Intellectual Property within the Exclusive Field of Use. Further, during the Term, except as otherwise provided herein, neither T&B nor any of its Affiliates may (1) offer to sell or sell any product or service that is the same or similar to the Products in the Exclusive Field of Use, or (2) contact, solicit, or direct any person or entity to contact or solicit, any of the customers of (or customers set forth in the Customer Data) for the purpose of providing any products or services that are the same or similar to the Products; provided, however, that T&B, in its sole and absolute discretion, shall have the right to terminate this Agreement, including all rights and licenses granted to LEA herein, if and as of the date that any monthly Royalty Payment (as defined in 9.3, below) payable to T&B does not exceed the Minimum Guaranteed Royalty for six (6) consecutive months.
perturbation.justification: The change in Section 1.2, which lowers the ownership threshold for defining an 'Affiliate' from 50% to 25%, directly contradicts Section 3.3. Section 3.3 restricts the actions of 'Affiliates' without specifying which definition of Affiliate to use. This creates ambiguity and a direct conflict as to whether entities with 25% ownership but less than 50% are bound by the restrictions outlined in Section 3.3, leading to potential disputes and uncertainty regarding the scope of the restrictions. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 1.10. The term "Products" shall mean any in-person remote (e.g., livestream of a live event, recording of a live event, and on-demand) product or service offerings in real estate investing strategies and techniques, asset protection, and entrepreneurship in any form of communication or media to one or more recipients, including, but not limited to, workshops, seminars, webinars, coaching, and mentorships, as may be offered by LEA in the conduct of the Business and for which a fee is charged by LEA.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.10. The term "Products" shall mean any in-person product or service offerings in real estate investing strategies and techniques, asset protection, and entrepreneurship in any form of communication or media to one or more recipients, including, but not limited to, workshops, seminars, webinars, coaching, and mentorships, as may be offered by LEA in the conduct of the Business and for which a fee is charged by LEA. The term shall specifically exclude any online remote access or materials, or access of any kind.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Products' is changed to exclude online materials. This directly contradicts section 7.2, where T&B can independently develop Products marketed and sold by T&B and fulfilled by T&B, and Cash Sales from these Products will be shared. Given the original text considered "Products" to include virtual courses as stated in the original 1.10 definition, this change introduces an in-text contradiction in the document, directly impacting compensation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.2 T&B Developed Products. In addition, T&B may independently develop Products to be marketed and sold by T&B and fulfilled by T&B. In lieu of any other royalty, the parties shall share Cash Sales from the sale of such independently developed T&B Products that are generated directly and independently by LEA as follows: [●%] to LEA [●%] to T&B
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Products' in section 1.10 explicitly excludes online remote access or materials. This directly contradicts section 7.2, which allows T&B to independently develop Products. The original text's inclusion of virtual courses in the definition of products meant T&B's independently developed Products could include virtual courses, and sales from those courses would be shared according to section 7.2. The changed definition, however, creates a contradiction because it removes online courses as Products, which impacts the products T&B can sell and the compensation received by both parties as dictated in section 7.2. This creates a direct conflict and violates the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 LEA shall provide administrative and operational services for the conduct of the Business, including, marketing, event planning, sales, operations, information technologies, human resources, and class fulfillment. In consultation with T&B and subject to the licenses granted to LEA by T&B herein, LEA shall be responsible for branding the Business (including trademarks and trade dress) and creating and producing marketing collateral, sales presentations, course materials and other tangible work product and deliverables related to the conduct of the Business (collectively, "Work Product").
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 LEA shall provide administrative and operational services for the conduct of the Business, including, marketing, event planning, sales, operations, information technologies, human resources, and class fulfillment. LEA shall be solely responsible for branding the Business (including trademarks and trade dress) and creating and producing marketing collateral, sales presentations, course materials and other tangible work product and deliverables related to the conduct of the Business (collectively, "Work Product"). T&B's consultation is optional and at LEA's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: Section 4.1 originally stated that LEA would brand the business in consultation with T&B. The modified text removes the consultation requirement and states that T&B's consultation is optional. This creates a direct in-text contradiction with Section 5.1, which states that T&B shall assist LEA in developing the business as reasonably requested. This creates ambiguity regarding T&B's role and influence in business development.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 T&B shall assist LEA in developing the Business as reasonably requested by LEA from time to time, including, but not limited to, assisting in the development of sales presentations and course materials and consultation with LEA's sales and marketing organization to ensure that they reflect T&B Personality's investing philosophy. T&B shall provide factual substantiation of T&B Personality's biography and investing success stories provided by T&B for use by LEA in the conduct of the Business.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 4.1 removes the mandatory consultation with T&B, making it optional. This directly contradicts Section 5.1, which states that T&B "shall assist LEA in developing the Business as reasonably requested". The word "shall" imposes a mandatory obligation on T&B to assist LEA. By making T&B's consultation optional, Section 4.1 now conflicts with T&B's obligation to assist as outlined in Section 5.1, creating a contradiction.
